project_overview.project_development_objective: To enhance the performance of the Ethio-Djibouti corridor through improvements in operational capacity, efficiency, and range of logistics services at the Modjo Dry Port
project_overview.beneficiaries: Private sector exporters, importers, manufacturers and farmers, those working for companies producing goods for export, government agencies involved in exporting and importing, and consumers

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: partial - recent unrest in Oromia region affecting internal security, state of emergency declared in October 2016

key_components.description: Investments in intermodal transfer facility, bulk storage and bagging facility, container yard and equipment, bonded and general warehousing, and consolidation/deconsolidation center

key_components.component_name: Enhancing coordination through investments in IT systems
key_components.description: Management information system for document flow between agencies and logistics terminal operation system at Modjo Dry Port
Capacity enhancement for EMAA, Logistics Transformation Office, and Ethiopia National Logistics Transformation Council, plus project management and M&E

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Ministry of Transport through Ethiopian Maritime Affairs Authority (EMAA)
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Project Steering Committee chaired by Minister of Transport, Technical Working Group, Project Implementation Unit within EMAA
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: International and National Competitive Bidding, consultant selection through QCBS and other methods per World Bank guidelines
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: PIU responsible for M&E system with quarterly progress reports to World Bank, dedicated M&E specialist to be hired

identified_risks.risk_description: Recent unrest in Oromia region affecting internal security, lack of effective political settlement mechanism
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Close monitoring of potential impact on project, necessary adjustments if needed

identified_risks.risk_description: EMAA has no experience implementing World Bank projects and limited staff with necessary skills
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Capacity building support, recruitment of additional personnel for PIU, training on World Bank requirements

identified_risks.risk_description: High staff turnover, shortage of qualified accountants and auditors, EMAA lacks experience with World Bank procurement
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Experienced project management staff for PIU, action plan to address FM and procurement weaknesses

identified_risks.risk_description: Resistance from government and private sector benefiting from current inefficiencies, coordination challenges with ESLSE
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Effective communications strategy, assess political economy issues, develop strategies to address resistance

identified_risks.risk_description: Deterioration of security situation could affect main project site in Modjo, Oromia
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Close monitoring during preparation and implementation

safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - project-level GRM building on Ethiopia's grievance redress systems, formalized, transparent, cost-effective and time-bound manner
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Ensure adequate gender representation in working groups and committees, special targeting for women-headed households in livelihood restoration, gender disaggregated data in M&E framework
vulnerable_groups: project affected persons from land acquisition

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project design considers recent unrest in Oromia region where main project site is located, state of emergency declared in October 2016
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Political tensions affecting internal security, lack of effective political settlement mechanism to peacefully moderate political tension
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Deterioration of security situation could potentially affect main project site at Modjo in Oromia region, close monitoring required
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - land acquisition conducted in 2014 affected 105 households, compensation paid but lacked livelihood restoration program, US$1 million allocated for livelihood restoration and rehabilitation
